description: Test cases for the UI/UX Design Consultant skill - analyzing and redesigning existing websites

evals.name: saas-landing-page-react
evals.prompt: I've got this Next.js SaaS landing page that looks pretty amateur. It's a project management tool called 'TaskFlow'. The main issues I see are: random spacing everywhere, like sometimes buttons have 8px padding and sometimes 24px for no reason. The colors are all over the place - we've got like 5 different shades of blue that don't match. Typography is a mess too, I think we're using 12 different font sizes. Can you audit this and help me make it look professional? The code is in /src with components in /src/components and the main page in /src/app/page.tsx. We're using Tailwind CSS.

must_include: Stack detection identifying Next.js and Tailwind
Discovery questions about product type, audience, goals
expected_output.format: Structured audit report following the template

evals.name: portfolio-html-redesign
evals.prompt: Hey, I have this portfolio website for my design work that I built a few years ago with plain HTML/CSS. It's pretty dated now - uses tables for layout in some places (I know, I know), has inconsistent fonts, and the colors are giving 2015 vibes. The project is at ./portfolio with index.html and a css/ folder. I want it to look modern and professional, something that would impress potential clients. Don't hold back on the critique. After you audit it, I want you to actually fix it - do the full redesign. I like clean, minimal aesthetics but nothing too boring.

must_include: Design direction selection (likely 'Clean Minimal' based on stated preference)
Actual code changes made to HTML and CSS files
expected_output.format: Complete workflow from audit through execution with changelog

evals.name: vue-dashboard-spacing-chaos
evals.prompt: We have a Vue 3 analytics dashboard built with Nuxt and it's got serious spacing inconsistency issues. Every component seems to have different padding - cards have random margins, the sidebar spacing doesn't match the main content, and the data tables look cramped in some places and way too airy in others. We're using a mix of inline styles and some CSS modules. I counted at least 30 different spacing values in the codebase. Can you run an audit focusing especially on the spacing/rhythm category? I need hard data on how bad it actually is and a plan to fix it by establishing a proper spacing system.

must_include: Spacing audit script output showing 30+ unique values
Spacing & Rhythm score with specific justification
Improvement plan with Tier 1 foundations (spacing system establishment)
Vue-specific guidance for applying the spacing system
expected_output.format: Audit with deep spacing analysis and remediation plan

evals.name: tailwind-component-library
evals.prompt: I'm working on a Tailwind-based component library for my startup. We've got buttons, cards, forms, modals - all the basics. Problem is, they were built by different developers at different times and nothing is consistent. Button sizes don't match input heights, hover states vary wildly, the focus rings are different colors, and our 'primary' color is defined 4 different ways across components. We're using React with TypeScript. I need you to audit the component design specifically, then help me establish proper design tokens in our tailwind.config.ts and make all components consistent. Work in batch mode once I approve the plan.

must_include: Component Design category scored with specific inconsistencies listed
Design tokens plan in tailwind.config.ts format
Batch mode execution with multiple components updated
expected_output.format: Component library harmonization with batch execution

evals.name: svelte-ecommerce-audit-only
evals.prompt: I need a design audit of our SvelteKit e-commerce site. We're using Tailwind and some custom SCSS. I don't want you to make any changes yet - just give me a thorough analysis of what's wrong and what needs to be fixed. Focus on the checkout flow pages especially. The site sells handmade jewelry so the aesthetic should feel premium and trustworthy. Give me the full 12-category scorecard with honest scores and specific examples of what's broken. I'll decide later if I want you to fix things.

must_include: SvelteKit detection with Tailwind and SCSS noted
Discovery questions about e-commerce context and checkout priorities
Specific examples from checkout flow pages
CTA & Conversion category emphasized given e-commerce context
Clear offer to proceed to Phase 3 if user wants improvement plan

assertions.description: Correctly identifies the framework and styling approach
assertions.check: Phase 0 output matches the actual stack described in the prompt

assertions.description: Asks appropriate discovery questions before auditing
assertions.check: At least 3 Tier 1 questions asked, adapted to context
Mentions specific data points from audit scripts (spacing values, color counts, etc.)

assertions.description: Requests explicit approval before code changes
assertions.check: Phase 4 approval gate executed before any file modifications

assertions.description: Makes changes using existing tools, no new dependencies
assertions.check: Code changes use the existing styling approach (Tailwind classes, CSS, etc.)

assertions.description: Establishes design tokens before component changes
assertions.check: Token file (tailwind.config or CSS variables) modified before component files
Phase 6 changelog includes score improvements, changes by section, files modified
